Class Book

java.lang.Object
Book
public class Book extends Object
Book public class. Holds all methods relating to the Book class and stores variables for each book. Variables stored include the title of the book, the borrowed status of the book, the name of the person who is borrowing the book (is nothing if not borrowed), and the price of the book.

    Description
    Book(String bookTitle, double bookPrice)

  • Constructor Details

    • Book

      public Book(String bookTitle, double bookPrice)
      Book constructor. Created with a bookTitle and a price for the book. Updates variables for the book title based on the input and the price of the book based on the input. Sets borrowed status to false by default and correspondingly sets the borrowerName to nothing since the book hasn't been borrowed yet.
      Parameters:
      bookTitle - The title of the book that is being created.
      bookPrice - The cost of the book in market price.

  • Method Details

    • borrowed

      public void borrowed(String userName)
      Changes the borrowing status of the book to true. Updates who borrowed the book based on the string input.
      Parameters:
      userName - Name of the user who is borrowing the book. Updates the book's borrowerName variable.

    • returned

      public void returned()
      Returns the book. Returns the book's borrowed and borrowerName status back to default, being false and nothing respectively.

    • isBorrowed

      public boolean isBorrowed()
      Returns the borrowing status of the book. This is based on the book's borrowed variable.
      Returns:
      this.borrowed which is either true or false depending on if the book has been borrowed.

    • getBorrowerName

      public String getBorrowerName()
      Gets the name of the borrower. Taken directly from the book's parameter.
      Returns:
      borrowerName parameter, which can be nothing or something depending on if the book is borrowed or not.

    • getTitle

      public String getTitle()
      Returns the title of the book. Used for comparison by other classes to check if a book is available or in a list.
      Returns:
      The title of the book from the book's parameters.

    • getPrice

      public double getPrice()
      Returns the original price of the book. This takes the book's price parameter.
      Returns:
      The book's original price.

    • calculatePayment

      public double calculatePayment(int days)
      Calculates the price that a user has to pay depending on how many days that the user borrowed the book. The price increases by 50 cents each day over 30 days that the book is borrowed. This is stored in a variable.
      Parameters:
      days - The number of days that the user has borrowed the book in total.
      Returns:
      payment, which is the amount of money the user owes to the library based on how many days that they borrowed the book.

    • main

      public static void main(String[] arguments)
      Main function. Holds test cases and defines variables for these test cases. Used to check if the functionality of book works as intended.
      Parameters:
      arguments - The input arguments from the command line.
